subject: [PATCH] cgroup/cpu: document cpu.stat nice_usec and core_sched.force_idle_usec

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Asked for clarification on the tree version used in the patch, specifically mentioning that he couldn't find the '(including those in descendant cgroups)' section anywhere. Otherwise, the change looks good.
reviews.raw_body:
> On Mon, Jul 20, 2026 at 08:02:26AM -0700, Joshua Hahn <joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com> wrote:
> > I have to ask, which tree is this based on? I can't seem to find a single
> > tree that contains the "(including those in descendant cgroups)" section
> > anywhere. 
> 
> It is most likely linux-next which contains a nearby modification from
> [1] (or it was based on that regardless).

Hello Michal,

Thank you, I must have missed linux-next in my searching... that makes
a lot of sense! With that, Tao, please feel free to add my review tag:

Reviewed-by: Joshua Hahn <joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com>

Thanks, have a great day!
Joshua

> HTH,
> Michal
> 
> [1] https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/20260629060636.200118-1-sunshaojie@kylinos.cn/

reviews.author: Michal Koutný
reviews.summary: Provided a helpful pointer to linux-next, which likely contains the necessary modification.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, Jul 20, 2026 at 08:02:26AM -0700, Joshua Hahn <joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com> wrote:
> I have to ask, which tree is this based on? I can't seem to find a single
> tree that contains the "(including those in descendant cgroups)" section
> anywhere. 

It is most likely linux-next which contains a nearby modification from
[1] (or it was based on that regardless).

HTH,
Michal

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/20260629060636.200118-1-sunshaojie@kylinos.cn/

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Added his review tag and thanked Michal for the pointer.

2026-07-20.patch_summary: Patch updates documentation for cgroup/cpu to include nice_usec and core_sched.force_idle_usec.
